themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to social services, housing, and public aid. This theme appears prominently in Spain, Italy, and Sweden, with parties like Vox, Lega, and Sverigedemokraterna advocating for these measures as a core electoral strategy.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox campaigns on 'national priority' in Andalusia, demanding Spaniards be prioritized for social aid and housing over immigrants.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega proposes extending 'national priority' policies to Lombardy, emphasizing local job protection and Italian-first hiring practices.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD advocates for stricter citizenship rules and prioritizing Swedish nationals in welfare access, aligning with national priority rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK proposes policies to prioritize British citizens in public services, echoing the 'national priority' frame.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN restricts family benefits and healthcare to French nationals, excluding migrants from social welfare programs.

themes.title: Trump’s Iran war dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Right-wing media in the U.S., Italy, Poland, and Hungary are intensely focused on Trump’s escalating tensions with Iran, including military operations in the Strait of Hormuz and diplomatic maneuvers. This theme reflects a shared concern over U.S. foreign policy and its implications for regional stability, with some European parties distancing themselves from Trump’s approach.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump announces 'Project Freedom' to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz amid rising tensions with Iran.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni distances Italy from Trump’s Iran policy, emphasizing NATO commitments and opposing U.S. troop withdrawals.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media covers Iran’s ultimatum to Trump and U.S. military operations, framing it as a critical geopolitical issue.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungarian coverage highlights Iran’s 30-day ultimatum to Trump, focusing on regional security implications.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK frames Iran tensions as a failure of global leadership, criticizing U.S. and EU responses.

themes.title: Meloni’s government longevity sparks cross-national debate
themes.blurb: Giorgia Meloni’s leadership in Italy is a focal point for right-wing and far-right parties in Europe, with debates over her government’s stability, policy decisions, and diplomatic relations with the U.S. and EU. This theme highlights how Meloni’s tenure serves as a reference point for evaluating right-wing governance across borders.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s government faces scrutiny over energy deals with Azerbaijan and diplomatic tensions with the U.S. over troop withdrawals.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD critiques Meloni’s EU policies, framing her leadership as a cautionary tale for right-wing governance.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN analyzes Meloni’s political strategies, particularly her handling of EU relations and migration policies.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media debates Meloni’s influence on European right-wing movements, comparing her policies to PiS’s domestic agenda.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungarian coverage assesses Meloni’s diplomatic maneuvers, particularly her stance on U.S. disengagement from Europe.

themes.title: Far-right surges in European polls and elections
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties in Germany, France, Sweden, and Norway are experiencing significant gains in polls and local elections, reflecting a broader trend of rising support for nationalist and anti-immigration platforms. This theme underscores the electoral momentum of far-right movements across Europe.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads polls in Germany with 28% support, capitalizing on economic discontent and anti-immigration sentiment.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN leads French presidential polls with 34-35% support, positioning itself as the dominant far-right force.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD gains traction in Sweden, advocating for stricter immigration policies and national sovereignty.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP maintains strong polling in Norway despite internal scandals, focusing on anti-immigration and tax-cut policies.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK surges in UK local elections, challenging Labour and Conservatives with nationalist policies.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right rhetoric in the U.S., UK, Italy, and Poland is increasingly linked to political violence, with parties and leaders facing criticism for inflammatory language that incites extremism. This theme highlights the transnational spread of violent political discourse and its consequences.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric is linked to rising political violence, including threats against journalists and opponents.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK’s anti-immigration rhetoric sparks backlash over plans to detain migrants in Green Party areas, seen as inciting division.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega’s anti-immigration stance fuels protests and clashes, particularly in Milan over housing policies.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS’s nationalist rhetoric contributes to internal party violence and public unrest, particularly during protests.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP faces backlash over racist remarks by advisors, sparking debates about the party’s role in normalizing extremism.
